name: Chandrayaan-3
fact: First spacecraft to land near the lunar south pole — the most contested territory in current space exploration, where permanently shadowed craters harbour water ice. Pragyan confirmed the presence of sulfur at the surface for the first time. India became the fourth nation to soft-land on the Moon, and the first to reach the poles.
capability: Polar landing technique — Mars's south pole region (where seasonal water ice exists) becomes a destination only after the polar landing problem is solved on the Moon.
